title_001: My Attachment Style — Who You Become When You Get Close
html_004: When you get close to someone, there’s a habit of the heart
html_005: that slips out before you even notice.
html_006: A dot moves across the radar in real time,
html_013: we’ve hidden which choice leads to which type
html_025: The way the dot drifts is just a hint — there are no right answers.
html_029: 💫 The you that comes out when you get close
html_032: 💌 Love like this and it gets easier
html_033: 🔒 Your attachment style isn’t a fixed fate.
html_034: Inside a safe relationship, we can always grow toward something more secure.
html_037: Just a fun little quiz. Whatever your type, your love has a temperature all its own 🤍
html_038: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_001: Even if their reply is a little late, I shrug it off as no big deal
js_002: When their reply is late, I can’t stop wondering if something’s wrong
js_003: I mostly trust that I’m loved, without second-guessing it
js_004: I often catch myself wanting to check that I’m still loved
js_005: Even after a fight, I trust we’ll be okay
js_006: After a fight, my chest tightens, afraid this is how we drift apart
js_007: If they’re too busy to make time, I’m let down but I get it
js_008: If they can’t make time, I wonder if they like me less
js_009: Being apart doesn’t make me feel the relationship is shaky
js_010: When we’re apart, I worry our hearts will drift too
js_011: I feel I can say what’s really on my mind without hurting us
js_012: If I say what I really feel, I worry it’ll be too much for them
js_013: A small shift in their tone doesn’t really rattle me
js_014: The slightest change in their tone puts me on high alert
js_015: When things are good, I just enjoy the moment
js_016: Even when things are good, part of me fears it’ll end someday
js_017: I trust them and give them plenty of space
js_018: I keep wanting to know what they’re up to, and end up checking
js_019: I don’t get anxious even if I’m not the one to reach out first
js_020: Afraid of being forgotten, I end up messaging first
js_021: I feel secure even without constant proof of love
js_022: I need to hear ‘I love you’ often to feel at ease
js_023: Even when I’m jealous, it doesn’t really throw me
js_024: Jealousy and comparison rock me pretty often
js_025: If we talk a little less for a while, I take it in stride
js_026: When we talk less, I get jittery that something’s wrong
js_027: Even picturing a breakup, I believe I’d be okay in the end
js_028: Just imagining being left behind is unbearable
js_029: When things are hard, I open up and lean on them
js_030: I tend to swallow the hard stuff and handle it on my own
js_031: I love a relationship that keeps getting closer and deeper
js_032: When it gets too close, I feel boxed in and need some distance
js_033: I tend to share my feelings often
js_034: I keep my feelings tucked away rather than show them
js_035: I want to spend lots of time together, just the two of us
js_036: I need enough time to myself to feel at ease
js_037: Asking them for help comes naturally to me
js_038: Leaning on anyone feels awkward and uncomfortable
js_039: The deeper the relationship, the more grounded I feel
js_040: When it gets deep, I feel like I’m losing my freedom
js_041: I tend to open up and show my inner world first
js_042: I rarely open up about what’s really inside
js_043: Picturing a future together gives me butterflies
js_044: Promising a future feels like a weight on me
js_045: I love trading little gestures of affection
js_046: Over-the-top affection makes me squirm and feels like too much
js_047: When conflict comes up, I sit down and try to work it out
js_048: When conflict comes up, I just want some space first
js_049: I’m glad when they step into my everyday life
js_050: When someone gets too far into my space, I get defensive
js_051: I can let them see me at my most vulnerable
js_052: I carry my weak moments alone and try not to show them
js_053: Even apart, I want to stay connected with frequent messages
js_054: When we’re apart, I’m comfortable doing our own thing
js_055: The feeling of ‘us’ is warm and lovely to me
js_056: Above all, my own independence matters most
js_058: “The one who grows calmer the closer you get”
js_059: You’re someone who can trust both yourself and your partner. You don’t fear closeness, yet you’re just as at ease with time alone. When conflict comes, you neither run nor cling — you say what you feel and work through it together.
js_060: Steady trust. You become a ‘safe harbor’ for your partner, giving love in a quiet, steady way rather than a loud one.
js_061: Not everyone is as at ease as you are. If you can wait for a partner who worries or keeps their distance, your bond will grow far deeper.
js_062: Keep loving as honestly and warmly as you do. Just know — if your partner has a different style, don’t hold back that one reassuring word.
js_064: “The one who wants more reassurance the closer you get”
js_065: You love deeply and fiercely. You feel most alive when hearts are connected, but a quiet phone or a lukewarm reply can unsettle you fast. Afraid of being left, you check in again and again, wanting to give more and receive more.
js_066: Fierce devotion and empathy. You read your partner’s feelings keenly and pour your whole heart into the relationship.
js_067: When anxiety swells, you can end up pushing your partner. When you feel yourself wobble, pause a beat and tell yourself, ‘this may be an old habit, not this moment.’
js_068: Express instead of check. Say it plainly — ‘I’m feeling anxious right now’ — and your partner hears trust, not pressure.
js_070: “The one who steps back the closer you get”
js_071: You’re independent and know how to protect yourself. You stand firm on your own, and a relationship that clings too tightly leaves you breathless. You’d rather solve things alone than lean, and you need time to show what you feel.
js_072: Self-reliance and calm. You respect your partner’s space and freedom, and you stay steady even in a crisis.
js_073: Keeping distance can feel like ‘pushing away’ to your partner. Just offering one small piece of your inner world first brings you much closer.
js_074: Don’t put off talking until you’ve got it perfectly sorted. Sharing even an unsorted heart, just a little, becomes intimacy itself.
js_076: “The one who’s scared up close, and longing from afar”
js_077: You long for love desperately and fear it at the same time. The wish to get close and the pull to retreat from hurt live side by side, so you often move in and back away again. That’s also how deeply, how fully, you feel a relationship.
js_078: Deep sensitivity and sincerity. You feel love’s bright side and its shadows alike, and you truly immerse yourself in a relationship.
js_079: Don’t blame yourself for the push and pull. It isn’t a flaw — it’s a habit that once kept your heart safe. Slowly building trust with someone who feels safe becomes a real source of strength.
js_080: When you feel scattered, one sentence instead of running. Saying ‘my heart’s tangled right now, I need a little time’ can spare you a lot of misunderstanding and regret.
